categories.title: Tutorials & Getting Started
categories.description: Entry points for researchers new to neuroimaging data analysis and social neuroscience methods.

links.name: Neuroimaging Analysis Replication and Prediction Study (NARPS)
links.blurb: How analytic choices shape fMRI results — a lesson in reproducibility.

links.name: Andy's Brain Book
links.blurb: Free, hands-on tutorials for FSL, AFNI, SPM, and fMRI analysis.

links.name: Neuromatch Academy — Computational Neuroscience
links.blurb: Open curriculum covering modelling, statistics, and neural data analysis.

links.name: Naturalistic Data Analysis
links.blurb: A community resource for analyzing movie- and narrative-based neuroimaging data.

categories.title: Preprocessing Pipelines
categories.description: Standardized, validated pipelines for turning raw acquisitions into analysis-ready data.

links.name: fMRIPrep
links.blurb: Robust, BIDS-native preprocessing pipeline for task and resting-state fMRI.

links.name: MRIQC
links.blurb: Automated quality-control metrics and reports for structural and functional MRI.

links.name: MNE-Python
links.blurb: Preprocessing and analysis of MEG, EEG, and iEEG data.

links.name: EEGLAB
links.blurb: MATLAB toolbox for EEG/MEG processing, central to the NEMAR ecosystem.

links.name: QSIPrep
links.blurb: Configurable preprocessing for diffusion MRI.

categories.title: BIDS — Brain Imaging Data Structure
categories.description: The community standard for organizing and describing neuroimaging datasets.

links.name: BIDS Specification
links.blurb: The formal, versioned standard for MRI, MEG, EEG, iEEG, and more.

links.name: BIDS Starter Kit
links.blurb: Tutorials, templates, and examples for getting your data BIDS-compliant.

links.name: BIDS Validator
links.blurb: Check whether a dataset conforms to the BIDS standard before sharing.

links.name: PyBIDS
links.blurb: Python library for querying and manipulating BIDS datasets.

categories.title: Repositories & Archives
categories.description: Where to find and deposit open neuroscience data.

links.name: OpenNeuro
links.blurb: Free hosting for BIDS-formatted MRI, MEG, EEG, and iEEG datasets.

links.name: DANDI Archive
links.blurb: Cellular neurophysiology data in the NWB standard.

links.name: NEMAR
links.blurb: EEG/MEG/iEEG archive with integrated compute via the Neuroscience Gateway.

links.name: NeuroVault
links.blurb: Repository for unthresholded statistical maps and atlases.

links.name: OpenNeuro Datasets on GitHub
links.blurb: Version-controlled mirrors of every OpenNeuro dataset.

categories.title: Data Sharing & FAIR Practices
categories.description: Guidance for sharing data responsibly, ethically, and reproducibly.

links.name: FAIR Guiding Principles
links.blurb: Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, Reusable — the foundation of open data.

links.name: DataLad
links.blurb: Git-based distributed data management used by many neuroimaging archives.

links.name: INCF — International Neuroinformatics Coordinating Facility
links.blurb: Standards and best practices for neuroscience data sharing.

links.name: NIH Data Management & Sharing Policy
links.blurb: Funder requirements that increasingly shape how data must be shared.

categories.title: Social Neuroscience Methods
categories.description: Tools and references specific to studying the social brain.

links.name: Inter-Subject Correlation (ISC) Toolbox
links.blurb: Analyze shared neural responses across people during naturalistic stimuli.

links.name: BrainIAK
links.blurb: Advanced fMRI methods including ISC, shared response models, and decoding.

links.name: Hyperscanning resources (fNIRS Society)
links.blurb: Methods and community for measuring two or more interacting brains.

links.name: Neurosynth
links.blurb: Large-scale automated meta-analysis of the fMRI literature, including social terms.
